sourceDesc.p: Derived from online catalogues of Hathi Trust, the Bodleian Library, the British Library, and the Library Hub

revisionDesc.change: Tagged in TEI and exposed on Digital Lacy website

body.p: This short bibliography lists in order of publication date most printed works attributed to Pascoe which are now available online. Some of these titles are effectively serials, reappearing annually with some modification but essentially the same content. The list does not include all such re-issues. Download links are included where possible. Where a title is not available online, I have indicated its shelf mark in the British Library, or a link to its entry in the Bodleian catalogue.
Please raise an issue on the Github repository if you spot any errors or omissions.

div.head: Published reference works and guides available online

bibl.title: The prohibitory law of Massachusetts versus the licensing law of England : with an account of the latter / by Charles E. Pascoe

A London directory for American travellers for 1874. Containing the fullest information, in the best form for reference, respecting all that is valuable in connection with a visit to London. With an appendix. By Charles E. Pascoe
bibl.publisher: Boston: Lee and Shepard; Lee, Shepard, and Dillingham, 1874

bibl.title: The illustrated handbook to the supplementary art galleries of London : spring exhibitions, 1877

listBibl.bibl: 1878A practical handbook to the principal professions : compiled from authentic sources, and based on the most recent regulations concerning admission to the Navy, Army, and Civil Services (Home and Indian), the legal and medical professions, the professions of a civil engineer, architect, and artist, and the mercantile marine / by Charles Eyre PascoeEngland: Hardwicke and Bogue, 1878[Reviews: Spectator, 1878-10 [Pall Mall Gazette, 1878-08-26]]

div.head: Pocket Guides and pamphlets not available online

bibl.title: Folkestone of to-day : an illustrated book for visitors; season 1889 / By Charles Eyre Pascoe. Illustrated by H. Tringham.
Eastbourne, Hastings, and St. Leonard's : a pocket-book of local information for visitors

div.head: Periodical contributions
div.p: Pascoe was a prolific contributor to magazines, both American and English. Many of these contributions (notably the two series contributed to the Leisure Hour) were subsequently recycled in a book or guide. Many titles mentioned elsewhere are yet to be traced. For those which have been found online, a link is provided below.

listBibl.bibl: “In Memoriam: Temple Bar.” Illus. New York: Appleton’s journal. vol 2 (1877) pp 75-81
“An English By-Lane”, New York: Appleton’s journal. Vol 1 (1876) pp 231-233
`Story of the English magazines’ Atlantic Monthly, (Sept 1884), pp 364-374. [1884_englishMagazines.pdf]
1884 `Buying a house’ The Leisure hour, Jan. 1877-Oct. 1903; London (May 1884): 294-298. [buyingHouse1884.pdf]

div.head: Newspaper correspondence
div.p: Pascoe was also an indefatigable writer of letters to the editor, frequently appearing as a harassed radical in The Daily News, which was the leading "Liberal" journal of his day.

listBibl.bibl: 1867 "The Reception of the Belgians." letter to the editor Royal Cornwall Gazette. (May 16, 1867) Issue 3331, p.6
1884 “American Payments to English Authors” letter in The Athenaeum. (Nov 8, 1884): p 592 Iss. 2976/
1886 "The Theatres" [quotes a letter from CEP] Daily News, (June 7, 1886) Iss. 12529
1889 “The Maybrick case” Daily News (Aug 13, 1889) Iss. 13526, p.2
